trivia_bullets.text: Only printing; from Throne of Eldraine (ELD, October 2019). One of two colorless-common artifact golems in ELD, the other being Clockwork Servant (no. 216). Associated with Garenbrig, the green-aligned court of Eldraine and home to the Great Henge.

"Everything that inhabits Garenbrig is affected by the wild magic flowing through the Great Henge." — no named speaker. The flavor connects the stone golem directly to the Great Henge as its animating source.

The Great Henge (ELD no. 161) is one of the most expensive and sought-after cards from Throne of Eldraine — a legendary artifact that generates mana, puts +1/+1 counters on creatures, and draws cards. Henge Walker is named for and flavored as a product of the same location. The cheap common golem and the expensive legendary artifact are the same place's two outputs.
sources: Scryfall: The Great Henge (ELD 161); WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Adamant (enters with a +1/+1 counter if at least three mana of the same color was spent) is ELD's set mechanic. As a colorless-cost creature, Henge Walker triggers Adamant with any single color's mana. EDHREC rank 25,605 — very low Commander presence. Legal in Pauper and Paupercommander.

Illustrated by Titus Lunter, known for sweeping landscape-focused MTG illustrations. The composition emphasizes the golem against the forest backdrop rather than close creature detail — consistent with Lunter's landscape-first framing style.
